TSMC will invest an additional $100 billion in the U.S. to build at least four more chipmaking plants and advanced packaging facilities in Arizona, CEO C.C. Wei announced at the company's second-quarter earnings conference in Taipei on Thursday. The new fabs will produce chips at the 2nm node and below, and the commitment lifts TSMC's total announced U.S. investment to $265 billion, confirming plans that appeared as a market rumor in February. TSMC declined to say when the additional $100 billion will be spent, but the commitment follows the U.S.-Taiwan trade agreement that cut tariffs on Taiwanese goods to 15% in exchange for $250 billion in planned Taiwanese investment in the U.S., roughly two weeks after President Donald Trump said TSMC was doubling the size of its Arizona operations. *Follow* *Tom's Hardware on Google News**, or* *add us as a preferred source**, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.*.","score":0.87807417,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.12news.com/article/news/local/arizona/tsmc-announces-100-billion-investment-in-phoenix-arizona/75-b7f52ab4-a283-449d-b7e6-0a392cdb5d61","title":"TSMC announces additional $100 billion investment in Arizona","content":"The company's total investment in Arizona has now been raised to $265 billion. committing $165 billion to building plants in the state.","score":0.8524574,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://tech-insider.org/tsmc-arizona-165-billion-expansion-gigafab-2026","title":"TSMC's $165B Arizona GigaFab: Reshaping US Chips [2026]","content":"The Taiwanese chipmaker’s aggressive expansion plans, which now include up to 12 fabrication plants and four advanced packaging facilities near Phoenix, signal a seismic shift in the global semiconductor supply chain – one that could reshape everything from AI chip production to US-Taiwan trade relations for decades to come. The first fab is already producing advanced chips for Apple and NVIDIA, the second fab’s construction is complete with equipment installation planned for Q3 2026, and the company has acquired over 2,000 acres of land near Phoenix to accommodate what industry analysts are calling a “GigaFab cluster.” With 2nm chip production now ramping in Taiwan and the company projecting $52–56 billion in capital expenditures for 2026 alone, TSMC’s Arizona bet is the centerpiece of a strategy to dominate the AI chip era while insulating against geopolitical risk. By building CoWoS capacity in Arizona, TSMC can offer customers a fully integrated manufacturing solution – from wafer fabrication to advanced packaging – without any chips needing to cross an ocean.","score":0.81524754,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.industrialinfo.com/news/article/chipmaker-tsmc-projects-2026-capex-will-reach-52-billion-56-billion--352061","title":"Chipmaker TSMC Projects 2026 Capex will Reach $52 Billion-$56 Bil...","content":"Check out our latest podcast episode on the outlook for European chemicals and transport fuels.
